heading: Sixto stage-angle packet is now reconstructed as an exact GeoGebra-style scene and passes family [56] validation
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto, geogebra, exact-scene, certification]
body: Built `sixto_geogebra_scene_export_v1.json`, `sixto_geogebra_adapter_cert.json`, `sixto_geogebra_reconstruction_summary.json`, and `build_sixto_geogebra_reconstruction.py` to pivot the Sixto lane from raster-first extraction to exact scene reconstruction. The reconstruction encodes the recovered internal stage-2/3/4 arm packet as three exact GeoGebra-style right triangles `tri_stage_02`, `tri_stage_03`, and `tri_stage_04` on the `qa_rational_pair_noreduce` substrate, using recovered stage centers and center-to-rim arm endpoints from the Sixto witness. The generated family [56] cert validates cleanly: schema PASS, determinism PASS, typed formation PASS for 3 objects, base algebra PASS, and step hash / exact RT / strict law PASS. Honest verdict: the Sixto stage-angle packet is now represented as an exact certified scene, so the raster is no longer the primary numerical object for that lane. The next work should extend this reconstruction to the time-distance graph or broader scene objects rather than squeezing more OCR from the image.
